availableCommands.name: debug
availableCommands.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues (bundled)

availableCommands.name: simplify
availableCommands.description: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: batch
availableCommands.description: Research and plan a large-scale change, then execute it in parallel across 5â€“30 isolated worktree agents that each open a PR. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: loop
availableCommands.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) (bundled)

availableCommands.name: claude-api
availableCommands.description:
Build apps with the Claude API or Anthropic SDK.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`/`claude_agent_sdk`, or user asks to use Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Agent SDK.
DO NOT TRIGGER when: code imports `openai`/other AI SDK, general programming, or ML/data-science tasks. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: gsd:plan-phase
availableCommands.description: Create detailed phase plan (PLAN.md) with verification loop (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:settings
availableCommands.description: Configure GSD workflow toggles and model profile (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:add-todo
availableCommands.description: Capture idea or task as todo from current conversation context (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:audit-milestone
availableCommands.description: Audit milestone completion against original intent before archiving (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:insert-phase
availableCommands.description: Insert urgent work as decimal phase (e.g., 72.1) between existing phases (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:research-phase
availableCommands.description: Research how to implement a phase (standalone - usually use /gsd:plan-phase instead) (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:join-discord
availableCommands.description: Join the GSD Discord community (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:discuss-phase
availableCommands.description: Gather phase context through adaptive questioning before planning (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:verify-work
availableCommands.description: Validate built features through conversational UAT (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:remove-phase
availableCommands.description: Remove a future phase from roadmap and renumber subsequent phases (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:debug
availableCommands.description: Systematic debugging with persistent state across context resets (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:add-phase
availableCommands.description: Add phase to end of current milestone in roadmap (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:update
availableCommands.description: Update GSD to latest version with changelog display (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:quick
availableCommands.description: Execute a quick task with GSD guarantees (atomic commits, state tracking) but skip optional agents (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:help
availableCommands.description: Show available GSD commands and usage guide (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:cleanup
availableCommands.description: Archive accumulated phase directories from completed milestones (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:progress
availableCommands.description: Check project progress, show context, and route to next action (execute or plan) (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:health
availableCommands.description: Diagnose planning directory health and optionally repair issues (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:resume-work
availableCommands.description: Resume work from previous session with full context restoration (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:reapply-patches
availableCommands.description: Reapply local modifications after a GSD update (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:execute-phase
availableCommands.description: Execute all plans in a phase with wave-based parallelization (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:complete-milestone
availableCommands.description: Archive completed milestone and prepare for next version (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:new-project
availableCommands.description: Initialize a new project with deep context gathering and PROJECT.md (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:pause-work
availableCommands.description: Create context handoff when pausing work mid-phase (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:set-profile
availableCommands.description: Switch model profile for GSD agents (quality/balanced/budget) (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:check-todos
availableCommands.description: List pending todos and select one to work on (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:map-codebase
availableCommands.description: Analyze codebase with parallel mapper agents to produce .planning/codebase/ documents (user)

input.hint: [optional: specific area to map, e.g., 'api' or 'auth']

availableCommands.name: gsd:list-phase-assumptions
availableCommands.description: Surface Claude's assumptions about a phase approach before planning (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:plan-milestone-gaps
availableCommands.description: Create phases to close all gaps identified by milestone audit (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:new-milestone
availableCommands.description: Start a new milestone cycle â€” update PROJECT.md and route to requirements (user)

availableCommands.name: create-workflow
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Formalize repeatable patterns into trackable kspec workflows. Design steps, choose triggers, create loop variants, and integrate with skills.

availableCommands.name: help
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Get help with kspec commands and workflows

availableCommands.name: observe
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Capture and act on systemic patterns â€” friction, successes, questions, and ideas. The feedback loop that drives process improvement.

availableCommands.name: plan
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Translate approved plans into specs and tasks. Import structured documents or create incrementally. Plans persist as durable artifacts with audit trail.

availableCommands.name: reflect
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Structured reflection at the end of work sessions. Identifies learnings, friction points, and improvements for system evolution.

availableCommands.name: review
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Kspec-specific review gates â€” spec alignment, own AC coverage, trait AC coverage, and validation integration. Building block for project-specific review workflows.

availableCommands.name: task-work
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Structured task lifecycle â€” start, work, note, submit, complete. Fix cycle handling, scope management, loop mode for automation, and quality gates.

availableCommands.name: triage
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Triage inbox items systematically. Records decisions with audit trail, then executes actions. Supports inbox, observations, and automation eligibility triage.

availableCommands.name: triage-automation
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Assess and prepare tasks for automation eligibility. Verify spec coverage, acceptance criteria, and task readiness for automated agents.

availableCommands.name: triage-inbox
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Process inbox items using the record-act pattern. Categorize, promote to spec/task, merge duplicates, defer, or delete stale items with full audit trail.

availableCommands.name: writing-specs
availableCommands.description: (kspec) Create and maintain specification items â€” modules, features, requirements, acceptance criteria, and traits. The source of truth for what to build.

availableCommands.name: compact
availableCommands.description: Clear conversation history but keep a summary in context. Optional: /compact [instructions for summarization]

availableCommands.name: context
availableCommands.description: Show current context usage

availableCommands.name: heapdump
availableCommands.description: Dump the JS heap to ~/Desktop

availableCommands.description: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation

availableCommands.description: Get comments from a GitHub pull request

availableCommands.description: Review a pull request

availableCommands.description: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

availableCommands.description: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions

Bug audit: PTY lifecycle and backend switching
â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
ULID:      01KKAWKTJVBTXZDAF2293N8875
Slugs:     bug-pty
Type:      bug
Status:    pending
Priority:  2
Automation: eligible

â”€â”€â”€ Description â”€â”€â”€
  Investigate and fix bugs in PTY session management. Check: PTY spawn error handling, resize debounce edge cases, output buffer overflow, backend switching race conditions, session ID preservation across switches, cleanup on unexpected PTY exit, enhanced PATH construction.
Spec ref:  @pty-lifecycle
Created:   2026-03-10T03:28:25.690Z

â”€â”€â”€ Spec Context â”€â”€â”€
PTY Session Lifecycle
Type: feature
Implementation: implemented
Description:
  Spawn, manage, resize, and terminate PTY processes running AI CLI backends. Ring buffer captures last 200 lines with ANSI stripping.
Acceptance Criteria:
  [ac-1]
    Given: user opens a project
    When: a terminal session is spawned
    Then: a PTY process is created with node-pty using the resolved backend executable, correct cwd, and xterm-256color terminal type
  [ac-2]
    Given: a PTY session is running
    When: terminal output is produced
    Then: output is captured in a 200-line ring buffer with ANSI codes stripped and relayed to renderer via IPC
  [ac-3]
    Given: a PTY session is running
    When: the user closes the tab or app exits
    Then: the PTY process is terminated and all listeners are cleaned up

content.text:
```console
OK Started task: 01KKAWKT

--- Spec Context ---
Implementing: PTY Session Lifecycle

Spawn, manage, resize, and terminate PTY processes running AI CLI backends. Ring buffer captures last 200 lines with ANSI stripping.

Acceptance Criteria (3):
  [ac-1]
    Given: user opens a project
    When: a terminal session is spawned
    Then: a PTY process is created with node-pty using the resolved backend executable, correct cwd, and xterm-256color terminal type
  [ac-2]
    Given: a PTY session is running
    When: terminal output is produced
    Then: output is captured in a 200-line ring buffer with ANSI codes stripped and relayed to renderer via IPC
  [ac-3]
    Given: a PTY session is running
    When: the user closes the tab or app exits
    Then: the PTY process is terminated and all listeners are cleaned up

Remember: Add test coverage for each AC and mark tests with // AC: @spec-ref ac-N

â„ą Synced spec "PTY Session Lifecycle" implementation: implemented -> in_progress
```

    29â†’    try {

    33â†’
    34â†’      const normalizedGlobalBackend = globalSettings.backend === 'default'
    35â†’        ? undefined
    36â†’        : globalSettings.backend
    37â†’

    39â†’      const effectiveBackend = normalizedBackend
    40â†’        || (project?.backend && project.backend !== 'default'
    41â†’          ? project.backend
    42â†’          : normalizedGlobalBackend || 'claude')
    43â†’
    44â†’      const pending = pendingApiPrompts.get(cwd)

     1â†’import { app } from 'electron'

     5â†’

    13â†’export interface Project {

    27â†’  categoryId?: string         // Category this project belongs to

    29â†’}
    30â†’

    74â†’    yellow?: string
    75â†’    blue?: string
    76â†’    magenta?: string
    77â†’    cyan?: string
    78â†’    white?: string

    81â†’
    82â†’export interface Settings {

    95â†’
    96â†’
    97â†’interface StoredData {
    98â†’  workspace: Workspace
    99â†’  windowBounds?: WindowBounds
   100â†’  settings?: Settings
   101â†’}
   102â†’
   103â†’export class SessionStore {
   104â†’  private configPath: string
   105â†’  private backupPath: string
   106â†’  private data: StoredData
   107â†’
   108â†’  constructor() {
   109â†’    const configDir = join(app.getPath('userData'), 'config')
   110â†’    if (!existsSync(configDir)) {
   111â†’      mkdirSync(configDir, { recursive: true })
   112â†’    }
   113â†’    this.configPath = join(configDir, 'workspace.json')
   114â†’    this.backupPath = join(configDir, 'workspace.json.backup')
   115â†’    this.data = this.load()
   116â†’  }
   117â†’
   118â†’  private loadFile(path: string): StoredData | null {
   119â†’    try {
   120â†’      if (!existsSync(path)) return null
   121â†’      const stat = statSync(path)
   122â†’      if (stat.size === 0) return null
   123â†’      const content = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')
   124â†’      const data = JSON.parse(content)
   125â†’      if (!data?.workspace) return null
   126â†’      return data
   127â†’    } catch (e) {
   128â†’      console.error(`[SessionStore] Failed to load ${path}:`, e)
   129â†’      return null
   130â†’    }
   131â†’  }
   132â†’
   133â†’  private load(): StoredData {
   134â†’    // Try main file first
   135â†’    const main = this.loadFile(this.configPath)
   136â†’    if (main && (main.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
   137â†’      return main
   138â†’    }
   139â†’
   140â†’    // Main file is empty/corrupt/missing â€” try backup
   141â†’    const backup = this.loadFile(this.backupPath)
   142â†’    if (backup && (backup.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
   143â†’      console.log('[SessionStore] Main config empty/corrupt, restored from backup (' +
   144â†’        backup.workspace.projects.length + ' projects)')
   145â†’      // Restore backup as main file immediately
   146â†’      try {
   147â†’        writeFileSync(this.configPath, JSON.stringify(backup, null, 2))
   148â†’      } catch (e) {
   149â†’        console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to restore backup to main:', e)
   150â†’      }
   151â†’      return backup
   152â†’    }
   153â†’
   154â†’    // If main loaded but had 0 projects (valid empty state), use it
   155â†’    if (main) return main
   156â†’
   157â†’    return {
   158â†’      workspace: {
   159â†’        projects: [],
   160â†’        openTabs: [],
   161â†’        activeTabId: null
   162â†’      }
   163â†’    }
   164â†’  }
   165â†’
   166â†’  private save(): void {
   167â†’    try {
   168â†’      const json = JSON.stringify(this.data, null, 2)
   169â†’
   170â†’      // Validate we can parse what we're about to write
   171â†’      JSON.parse(json)
   172â†’
   173â†’      // Back up existing file before overwriting (if it has content)
   174â†’      if (existsSync(this.configPath)) {
   175â†’        try {
   176â†’          const stat = statSync(this.configPath)
   177â†’          if (stat.size > 0) {
   178â†’            // Only update backup if existing file is valid and has projects
   179â†’            const existing = this.loadFile(this.configPath)
   180â†’            if (existing && (existing.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
   181â†’              writeFileSync(this.backupPath, readFileSync(this.configPath))
   182â†’            }
   183â†’          }
   184â†’        } catch (e) {
   185â†’          console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to update backup:', e)
   186â†’        }
   187â†’      }
   188â†’
   189â†’      // Atomic write: write to temp file, then rename
   190â†’      const tmpPath = this.configPath + '.tmp'
   191â†’      writeFileSync(tmpPath, json)
   192â†’
   193â†’      // Verify the temp file was written completely
   194â†’      const written = readFileSync(tmpPath, 'utf-8')
   195â†’      JSON.parse(written) // throws if truncated/corrupt
   196â†’
   197â†’      renameSync(tmpPath, this.configPath)
   198â†’    } catch (e) {
   199â†’      console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to save workspace:', e)
   200â†’    }
   201â†’  }
   202â†’
   203â†’  getWorkspace(): Workspace {
   204â†’    return this.data.workspace
   205â†’  }
   206â†’
   207â†’  // Reload workspace from disk (useful when file was modified externally)
   208â†’  reloadFromDisk(): void {
   209â†’    console.log('[SessionStore] Reloading workspace from disk')
   210â†’    this.data = this.load()
   211â†’    console.log('[SessionStore] Reloaded, projects:', this.data.workspace?.projects?.length || 0)
   212â†’  }
   213â†’
   214â†’  saveWorkspace(workspace: Workspace): void {
   215â†’    // Protect against overwriting populated workspace with empty one
   216â†’    const incomingProjects = workspace?.projects?.length || 0
   217â†’    const currentProjects = this.data.workspace?.projects?.length || 0
   218â†’    if (incomingProjects === 0 && currentProjects > 0) {
   219â†’      console.log('[SessionStore] Rejected empty workspace save - current has', currentProjects, 'projects')
   220â†’      return
   221â†’    }
   222â†’    this.data.workspace = workspace
   223â†’    this.save()
   224â†’    syncMetaProjects(workspace)
   225â†’  }
   226â†’
   227â†’  getWindowBounds(): WindowBounds | undefined {
   228â†’    return this.data.windowBounds
   229â†’  }
   230â†’
   231â†’  saveWindowBounds(bounds: WindowBounds): void {
   232â†’    this.data.windowBounds = bounds
   233â†’    this.save()
   234â†’  }
   235â†’
   236â†’  getSettings(): Settings {

   238â†’  }
   239â†’
   240â†’  saveSettings(settings: Settings): void {

   244â†’}

     3â†’type Backend = 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider'
     4â†’
     5â†’export const ptyHandlers = {
     6â†’  spawnPty: (cwd: string, sessionId?: string, model?: string, backend?: Backend): Promise<string> =>
     7â†’    ipcRenderer.invoke('pty:spawn', { cwd, sessionId, model, backend }),
     8â†’
     9â†’  writePty: (id: string, data: string): void => ipcRenderer.send('pty:write', { id, data }),
    10â†’
    11â†’  resizePty: (id: string, cols: number, rows: number): void => ipcRenderer.send('pty:resize', { id, cols, rows }),
    12â†’
    13â†’  killPty: (id: string): void => ipcRenderer.send('pty:kill', id),
    14â†’
    15â†’  setPtyBackend: (id: string, backend: Backend): Promise<void> =>
    16â†’    ipcRenderer.invoke('pty:set-backend', { id, backend }),
    17â†’
    18â†’  onPtyData: (id: string, callback: (data: string) => void): (() => void) => {
    19â†’    const handler = (_: IpcRendererEvent, data: string) => callback(data)
    20â†’    ipcRenderer.on(`pty:data:${id}`, handler)
    21â†’    return () => ipcRenderer.removeListener(`pty:data:${id}`, handler)
    22â†’  },
    23â†’
    24â†’  onPtyExit: (id: string, callback: (code: number) => void): (() => void) => {
    25â†’    const handler = (_: IpcRendererEvent, code: number) => callback(code)
    26â†’    ipcRenderer.on(`pty:exit:${id}`, handler)
    27â†’    return () => ipcRenderer.removeListener(`pty:exit:${id}`, handler)
    28â†’  },
    29â†’
    30â†’  onPtyRecreated: (callback: (data: { oldId: string; newId: string; backend: Backend }) => void): (() => void) => {
    31â†’    const handler = (_: IpcRendererEvent, data: { oldId: string; newId: string; backend: Backend }) => callback(data)
    32â†’    ipcRenderer.on('pty:recreated', handler)
    33â†’    return () => ipcRenderer.removeListener('pty:recreated', handler)
    34â†’  }
    35â†’}
    36â†’

1â†’/**
     2â†’ * PTY WebSocket Management
     3â†’ *
     4â†’ * Manages WebSocket connections for PTY data streaming.
     5â†’ */
     6â†’
     7â†’import { MAX_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS, RECONNECT_DELAYS } from './constants'
     8â†’import { PtyWebSocketState } from './types'
     9â†’
    10â†’export class PtyWebSocketManager {
    11â†’  private ptyWebsockets: Map<string, PtyWebSocketState> = new Map()
    12â†’  private wsBaseUrl: string
    13â†’  private token: string
    14â†’
    15â†’  constructor(wsBaseUrl: string, token: string) {
    16â†’    this.wsBaseUrl = wsBaseUrl
    17â†’    this.token = token
    18â†’  }
    19â†’
    20â†’  getPtyWebsockets(): Map<string, PtyWebSocketState> {
    21â†’    return this.ptyWebsockets
    22â†’  }
    23â†’
    24â†’  /**
    25â†’   * Connect WebSocket for PTY data streaming
    26â†’   */
    27â†’  connectPtyStream(ptyId: string): void {
    28â†’    // Don't reconnect if already connected or connecting
    29â†’    const existing = this.ptyWebsockets.get(ptyId)
    30â†’    if (
    31â†’      existing?.ws &&
    32â†’      (existing.ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN || existing.ws.readyState === WebSocket.CONNECTING)
    33â†’    ) {
    34â†’      return
    35â†’    }
    36â†’
    37â†’    const url = `${this.wsBaseUrl}/api/pty/${ptyId}/stream?token=${encodeURIComponent(this.token)}`
    38â†’    console.log('[HttpBackend] Connecting PTY stream:', ptyId)
    39â†’
    40â†’    const ws = new WebSocket(url)
    41â†’
    42â†’    // Reuse existing state if available (preserves callbacks), otherwise create new
    43â†’    const state: PtyWebSocketState = existing || {
    44â†’      ws,
    45â†’      dataCallbacks: new Set(),
    46â†’      exitCallbacks: new Set(),
    47â†’      reconnectAttempts: 0,
    48â†’      reconnectTimer: null,
    49â†’      dataBuffer: []
    50â†’    }
    51â†’    // Update the WebSocket reference
    52â†’    state.ws = ws
    53â†’    state.reconnectAttempts = existing?.reconnectAttempts || 0
    54â†’
    55â†’    ws.onopen = () => {
    56â†’      console.log('[HttpBackend] PTY stream connected:', ptyId)
    57â†’      state.reconnectAttempts = 0
    58â†’    }
    59â†’
    60â†’    ws.onmessage = (event) => {
    61â†’      try {
    62â†’        const msg = JSON.parse(event.data)
    63â†’
    64â†’        switch (msg.type) {
    65â†’          case 'data':
    66â†’            // Buffer data if no callbacks registered yet
    67â†’            if (state.dataCallbacks.size === 0) {
    68â†’              state.dataBuffer.push(msg.data)
    69â†’              // Limit buffer size to prevent memory issues
    70â†’              if (state.dataBuffer.length > 1000) {
    71â†’                state.dataBuffer.shift()
    72â†’              }
    73â†’            } else {
    74â†’              // Forward data to all callbacks
    75â†’              state.dataCallbacks.forEach((cb) => cb(msg.data))
    76â†’            }
    77â†’            break
    78â†’
    79â†’          case 'exit':
    80â†’            // Forward exit to all callbacks
    81â†’            state.exitCallbacks.forEach((cb) => cb(msg.code))
    82â†’            // Clean up after exit
    83â†’            this.ptyWebsockets.delete(ptyId)
    84â†’            break
    85â†’
    86â†’          case 'connected':
    87â†’            console.log(
    88â†’              '[HttpBackend] PTY stream confirmed:',
    89â†’              ptyId,
    90â†’              'callbacks:',
    91â†’              state.dataCallbacks.size,
    92â†’              'buffered:',
    93â†’              state.dataBuffer.length
    94â†’            )
    95â†’            break
    96â†’
    97â†’          case 'pong':
    98â†’            // Keep-alive response, nothing to do
    99â†’            break
   100â†’
   101â†’          default:
   102â†’            console.log('[HttpBackend] Unknown PTY message type:', msg.type)
   103â†’        }
   104â†’      } catch (e) {
   105â†’        console.error('[HttpBackend] Failed to parse PTY message:', e)
   106â†’      }
   107â†’    }
   108â†’
   109â†’    ws.onerror = (event) => {
   110â†’      console.error('[HttpBackend] PTY stream error:', ptyId, event)
   111â†’    }
   112â†’
   113â†’    ws.onclose = (event) => {
   114â†’      console.log('[HttpBackend] PTY stream closed:', ptyId, event.code, event.reason)
   115â†’
   116â†’      // Attempt reconnection if not a clean close and we still want this PTY
   117â†’      if (!event.wasClean && state.reconnectAttempts < MAX_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS) {
   118â†’        const delay =

   120â†’        console.log(`[HttpBackend] Reconnecting PTY stream in ${delay}ms...`)

   124â†’          this.connectPtyStream(ptyId)

     1â†’/**
     2â†’ * PTY WebSocket Management
     3â†’ *
     4â†’ * Manages WebSocket connections for PTY data streaming.
     5â†’ */
     6â†’
     7â†’import { MAX_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS, RECONNECT_DELAYS } from './constants'
     8â†’import { PtyWebSocketState } from './types'
     9â†’
    10â†’export class PtyWebSocketManager {
    11â†’  private ptyWebsockets: Map<string, PtyWebSocketState> = new Map()
    12â†’  private wsBaseUrl: string
    13â†’  private token: string
    14â†’
    15â†’  constructor(wsBaseUrl: string, token: string) {
    16â†’    this.wsBaseUrl = wsBaseUrl
    17â†’    this.token = token
    18â†’  }
    19â†’
    20â†’  getPtyWebsockets(): Map<string, PtyWebSocketState> {
    21â†’    return this.ptyWebsockets
    22â†’  }
    23â†’
    24â†’  /**
    25â†’   * Connect WebSocket for PTY data streaming
    26â†’   */
    27â†’  connectPtyStream(ptyId: string): void {
    28â†’    // Don't reconnect if already connected or connecting
    29â†’    const existing = this.ptyWebsockets.get(ptyId)
    30â†’    if (
    31â†’      existing?.ws &&
    32â†’      (existing.ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN || existing.ws.readyState === WebSocket.CONNECTING)
    33â†’    ) {
    34â†’      return
    35â†’    }
    36â†’
    37â†’    const url = `${this.wsBaseUrl}/api/pty/${ptyId}/stream?token=${encodeURIComponent(this.token)}`
    38â†’    console.log('[HttpBackend] Connecting PTY stream:', ptyId)
    39â†’
    40â†’    const ws = new WebSocket(url)
    41â†’
    42â†’    // Reuse existing state if available (preserves callbacks), otherwise create new
    43â†’    const state: PtyWebSocketState = existing || {
    44â†’      ws,
    45â†’      dataCallbacks: new Set(),
    46â†’      exitCallbacks: new Set(),
    47â†’      reconnectAttempts: 0,
    48â†’      reconnectTimer: null,
    49â†’      dataBuffer: []
    50â†’    }
    51â†’    // Update the WebSocket reference
    52â†’    state.ws = ws
    53â†’    state.reconnectAttempts = existing?.reconnectAttempts || 0
    54â†’
    55â†’    ws.onopen = () => {
    56â†’      console.log('[HttpBackend] PTY stream connected:', ptyId)
    57â†’      state.reconnectAttempts = 0
    58â†’    }
    59â†’
    60â†’    ws.onmessage = (event) => {
    61â†’      try {
    62â†’        const msg = JSON.parse(event.data)
    63â†’
    64â†’        switch (msg.type) {
    65â†’          case 'data':
    66â†’            // Buffer data if no callbacks registered yet
    67â†’            if (state.dataCallbacks.size === 0) {
    68â†’              state.dataBuffer.push(msg.data)
    69â†’              // Limit buffer size to prevent memory issues
    70â†’              if (state.dataBuffer.length > 1000) {
    71â†’                state.dataBuffer.shift()
    72â†’              }
    73â†’            } else {
    74â†’              // Forward data to all callbacks
    75â†’              state.dataCallbacks.forEach((cb) => cb(msg.data))
    76â†’            }
    77â†’            break
    78â†’
    79â†’          case 'exit':
    80â†’            // Forward exit to all callbacks
    81â†’            state.exitCallbacks.forEach((cb) => cb(msg.code))
    82â†’            // Clean up after exit
    83â†’            this.ptyWebsockets.delete(ptyId)
    84â†’            break
    85â†’
    86â†’          case 'connected':
    87â†’            console.log(
    88â†’              '[HttpBackend] PTY stream confirmed:',
    89â†’              ptyId,
    90â†’              'callbacks:',
    91â†’              state.dataCallbacks.size,
    92â†’              'buffered:',
    93â†’              state.dataBuffer.length
    94â†’            )
    95â†’            break
    96â†’
    97â†’          case 'pong':
    98â†’            // Keep-alive response, nothing to do
    99â†’            break
   100â†’
   101â†’          default:
   102â†’            console.log('[HttpBackend] Unknown PTY message type:', msg.type)
   103â†’        }
   104â†’      } catch (e) {
   105â†’        console.error('[HttpBackend] Failed to parse PTY message:', e)
   106â†’      }
   107â†’    }
   108â†’
   109â†’    ws.onerror = (event) => {
   110â†’      console.error('[HttpBackend] PTY stream error:', ptyId, event)
   111â†’    }
   112â†’
   113â†’    ws.onclose = (event) => {
   114â†’      console.log('[HttpBackend] PTY stream closed:', ptyId, event.code, event.reason)
   115â†’
   116â†’      // Attempt reconnection if not a clean close and we still want this PTY
   117â†’      if (!event.wasClean && state.reconnectAttempts < MAX_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS) {

   119â†’          RECONNECT_DELAYS[Math.min(state.reconnectAttempts, RECONNECT_DELAYS.length - 1)]
   120â†’        console.log(`[HttpBackend] Reconnecting PTY stream in ${delay}ms...`)
   121â†’
   122â†’        state.reconnectTimer = setTimeout(() => {
   123â†’          state.reconnectAttempts++
   124â†’          this.connectPtyStream(ptyId)
   125â†’        }, delay)
   126â†’      } else if (event.code !== 1000) {
   127â†’        // Exit callback for abnormal closure
   128â†’        state.exitCallbacks.forEach((cb) => cb(-1))
   129â†’        this.ptyWebsockets.delete(ptyId)
   130â†’      }
   131â†’    }
   132â†’
   133â†’    this.ptyWebsockets.set(ptyId, state)
   134â†’  }
   135â†’
   136â†’  /**

   141â†’    if (!state) return

   144â†’    if (state.reconnectTimer) {
   145â†’      clearTimeout(state.reconnectTimer)
   146â†’    }
   147â†’
   148â†’    // Close WebSocket
   149â†’    if (
   150â†’      state.ws &&
   151â†’      (state.ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN || state.ws.readyState === WebSocket.CONNECTING)
   152â†’    ) {
   153â†’      state.ws.close(1000, 'Client requested close')
   154â†’    }
   155â†’
   156â†’    this.ptyWebsockets.delete(ptyId)
   157â†’  }
   158â†’
   159â†’  /**
   160â†’   * Disconnect all PTY WebSocket connections
   161â†’   */
   162â†’  disconnectAll(): void {
   163â†’    this.ptyWebsockets.forEach((state, ptyId) => {
   164â†’      if (state.reconnectTimer) {
   165â†’        clearTimeout(state.reconnectTimer)
   166â†’      }
   167â†’      if (
   168â†’        state.ws &&
   169â†’        (state.ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN || state.ws.readyState === WebSocket.CONNECTING)

   171â†’        state.ws.close(1000, 'Client disconnecting')
   172â†’      }
   173â†’    })
   174â†’    this.ptyWebsockets.clear()
   175â†’  }
   176â†’
   177â†’  /**

   180â†’  sendToPty(ptyId: string, message: object): boolean {

     1â†’import React, { useEffect, useState, useCallback, useRef } from 'react'
     2â†’import '@xterm/xterm/css/xterm.css'
     3â†’import { TerminalBar } from '../TerminalBar.js'
     4â†’import { AutoWorkOptions } from '../TerminalMenu.js'
     5â†’import { CustomCommandModal } from '../CustomCommandModal.js'
     6â†’import { resolveBackendCommand } from '../../utils/backendCommands.js'
     7â†’import type { TerminalProps } from './types.js'
     8â†’import { useTerminalSetup } from './useTerminalSetup.js'
     9â†’import { useTTS } from './useTTS.js'
    10â†’import { useAutoWork } from './useAutoWork.js'
    11â†’import { useSummaryCapture } from './useSummaryCapture.js'
    12â†’import { clearTerminalBuffer, cleanupOrphanedBuffers, formatPathsForBackend } from './utils.js'
    13â†’
    14â†’// Re-export buffer utilities for external use
    15â†’export { clearTerminalBuffer, cleanupOrphanedBuffers }
    16â†’
    17â†’/**
    18â†’ * Terminal component that wraps xterm.js with PTY integration.
    19â†’ * Supports TTS, auto work loop, summary capture, and backend-specific commands.
    20â†’ */
    21â†’export function Terminal({ ptyId, isActive, theme, onFocus, projectPath, backend, api, isMobile, onOpenFileBrowser }: TerminalProps): React.ReactElement {
    22â†’  // Custom command modal state
    23â†’  const [showCustomCommandModal, setShowCustomCommandModal] = useState(false)
    24â†’
    25â†’  // PTY write helper - uses api if provided, otherwise window.electronAPI
    26â†’  const writePty = useCallback((id: string, data: string) => {
    27â†’    if (api) {
    28â†’      api.writePty(id, data)
    29â†’    } else {
    30â†’      window.electronAPI?.writePty(id, data)
    31â†’    }
    32â†’  }, [api])
    33â†’
    34â†’  // Backend change handler
    35â†’  const handleBackendChange = useCallback((newBackend: 'default' | 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider') => {
    36â†’    if (newBackend === 'default') return
    37â†’    api?.setPtyBackend?.(ptyId, newBackend)
    38â†’    window.electronAPI?.setPtyBackend?.(ptyId, newBackend)
    39â†’  }, [api, ptyId])
    40â†’
    41â†’  // Send backend-specific command
    42â†’  const sendBackendCommand = useCallback((commandId: string): boolean => {
    43â†’    const backendCommand = resolveBackendCommand(backend, commandId)
    44â†’    if (!backendCommand) {
    45â†’      return false
    46â†’    }
    47â†’    writePty(ptyId, backendCommand)
    48â†’    setTimeout(() => {
    49â†’      writePty(ptyId, '\r')
    50â†’    }, 100)
    51â†’    return true
    52â†’  }, [backend, ptyId, writePty])
    53â†’
    54â†’  // TTS hook
    55â†’  const {
    56â†’    processTTSChunk,
    57â†’    handleUserInput,
    58â†’    resetTTSState,
    59â†’    prePopulateSpokenContent,
    60â†’  } = useTTS({ ptyId, isActive })
    61â†’
    62â†’  // Auto work hook (needs summary capture's triggerSummarize)
    63â†’  // We'll wire it up after summary capture is created
    64â†’  const autoWorkHookPlaceholder = useAutoWork({
    65â†’    ptyId,
    66â†’    sendBackendCommand,
    67â†’    triggerSummarize: () => {}, // Will be replaced
    68â†’  })
    69â†’
    70â†’  // Summary capture hook
    71â†’  const {
    72â†’    processSummaryChunk,
    73â†’    triggerSummarize,
    74â†’  } = useSummaryCapture({
    75â†’    ptyId,
    76â†’    sendBackendCommand,
    77â†’    autoWorkWithSummary: autoWorkHookPlaceholder.autoWorkState.withSummary,
    78â†’    buildAutoWorkPrompt: autoWorkHookPlaceholder.buildAutoWorkPrompt,
    79â†’  })
    80â†’
    81â†’  // Reconnect auto work with actual triggerSummarize
    82â†’  const {
    83â†’    autoWorkState,
    84â†’    awaitingUserReview,
    85â†’    handleAutoWorkMarker,
    86â†’    startAutoWork,
    87â†’    continueAutoWork,
    88â†’    stopAutoWork,
    89â†’    cancelAutoWork,
    90â†’  } = useAutoWork({
    91â†’    ptyId,
    92â†’    sendBackendCommand,
    93â†’    triggerSummarize,
    94â†’  })
    95â†’
    96â†’  // Terminal setup hook
    97â†’  const {
    98â†’    containerRef,
    99â†’    terminalRef,
   100â†’    fitAddonRef,
   101â†’    userScrolledUpRef,
   102â†’    currentLineInputRef,
   103â†’    inputSuppressedRef,
   104â†’  } = useTerminalSetup({
   105â†’    ptyId,
   106â†’    theme,
   107â†’    backend,
   108â†’    api,
   109â†’    onTTSChunk: processTTSChunk,
   110â†’    onUserInput: handleUserInput,
   111â†’    onSummaryChunk: processSummaryChunk,
   112â†’    onAutoWorkMarker: handleAutoWorkMarker,
   113â†’    prePopulateSpokenContent,
   114â†’    resetTTSState,
   115â†’  })
   116â†’
   117â†’  // Refit when tab becomes active
   118â†’  useEffect(() => {
   119â†’    if (isActive && fitAddonRef.current && containerRef.current && terminalRef.current) {
   120â†’      const doFit = () => {
   121â†’        if (!fitAddonRef.current || !containerRef.current || !terminalRef.current) return
   122â†’
   123â†’        const rect = containerRef.current.getBoundingClientRect()
   124â†’        if (rect.width > 50 && rect.height > 50) {
   125â†’          const wasAtBottom = !userScrolledUpRef.current
   126â†’
   127â†’          fitAddonRef.current.fit()
   128â†’          const dims = fitAddonRef.current.proposeDimensions()
   129â†’          if (dims && dims.cols > 0 && dims.rows > 0) {
   130â†’            window.electronAPI?.resizePty(ptyId, dims.cols, dims.rows)
   131â†’          }
   132â†’          if (wasAtBottom) {
   133â†’            requestAnimationFrame(() => {
   134â†’              terminalRef.current?.scrollToBottom()
   135â†’            })
   136â†’          }
   137â†’        }
   138â†’        terminalRef.current?.focus()
   139â†’      }
   140â†’
   141â†’      requestAnimationFrame(doFit)
   142â†’      setTimeout(doFit, 50)
   143â†’      setTimeout(doFit, 150)
   144â†’    }
   145â†’  }, [isActive, ptyId, containerRef, terminalRef, fitAddonRef, userScrolledUpRef])
   146â†’
   147â†’  // Handle file drop from file manager
   148â†’  const handleDrop = useCallback((e: React.DragEvent) => {
   149â†’    e.preventDefault()
   150â†’    e.stopPropagation()

   316â†’
   317â†’  async refresh(): Promise<void> {
   318â†’    this.checkApi()
   319â†’    return window.electronAPI!.refresh()
   320â†’  }
   321â†’
   322â†’  async openExternal(url: string): Promise<void> {
   323â†’    this.checkApi()
   324â†’    return window.electronAPI!.openExternal(url)
   325â†’  }
   326â†’
   327â†’  debugLog(message: string): void {
   328â†’    this.checkApi()
   329â†’    window.electronAPI!.debugLog(message)
   330â†’  }
   331â†’}
   332â†’
   333â†’/**
   334â†’ * Singleton instance of the Electron backend
   335â†’ */
   336â†’let instance: ElectronBackend | null = null
   337â†’
   338â†’/**
   339â†’ * Get the singleton instance of the Electron backend
   340â†’ */
   341â†’export function getElectronBackend(): ElectronBackend {
   342â†’  if (!instance) {
   343â†’    instance = new ElectronBackend()
   344â†’  }
   345â†’  return instance
   346â†’}
   347â†’
   348â†’/**
   349â†’ * Check if the Electron API is available
   350â†’ */
   351â†’export function isElectronAvailable(): boolean {
   352â†’  return typeof window !== 'undefined' && !!window.electronAPI
   353â†’}
   354â†’

1â†’/**

     7â†’
     8â†’// ============================================================================

    11â†’

    22â†’// ============================================================================

    49â†’

    66â†’

    86â†’  apiSessionMode?: 'existing' | 'new-keep' | 'new-close'
    87â†’  apiModel?: 'default' | 'opus' | 'sonnet' | 'haiku'
    88â†’  autoAcceptTools?: string[]
    89â†’  permissionMode?: string

    97â†’
    98â†’/**
    99â†’ * Open tab representing an active terminal session
   100â†’ */

   152â†’// ============================================================================
   153â†’// API Interface
   154â†’// ============================================================================
   155â†’
   156â†’export type Unsubscribe = () => void

   173â†’/**
   174â†’ * Core API interface for the renderer
   175â†’ */
   176â†’export interface Api {
   177â†’  // Optional: Desktop-only voice catalog and XTTS management
   178â†’  voiceGetInstalled?: () => Promise<Array<{ key: string; displayName: string; source: 'builtin' | 'downloaded' | 'custom'; quality?: string; language?: string }>>
   179â†’  xttsGetVoices?: () => Promise<Array<{ id: string; name: string; language: string; createdAt: number }>>

   181â†’  voiceCheckWhisper?: () => Promise<{ installed: boolean; models: string[]; currentModel: string | null }>
   182â†’  voiceCheckTTS?: () => Promise<{ installed: boolean; engine: string | null; voices: string[]; currentVoice: string | null }>
   183â†’  voiceInstallWhisper?: (model: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
   184â†’  voiceApplySettings?: (settings: { ttsVoice?: string; ttsEngine?: string; ttsSpeed?: number; xttsTemperature?: number; xttsTopK?: number; xttsTopP?: number; xttsRepetitionPenalty?: number }) => Promise<{ success: boolean }>
   185â†’  voiceSetVoice?: (voice: string | { voice: string; engine: 'piper' | 'xtts' }) => Promise<{ success: boolean }>
   186â†’  ttsRemoveInstructions?: (projectPath: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean }>
   187â†’  extensionsGetInstalled?: () => Promise<Array<{ id: string; name: string; type: string }>>
   188â†’
   189â†’  // Optional: API server control (desktop only)
   190â†’  apiStart?: (projectPath: string, port: number) => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }>
   191â†’  apiStop?: (projectPath: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean }>
   192â†’
   193â†’  // ==========================================================================
   194â†’  // Workspace Management
   195â†’  // ==========================================================================
   196â†’
   197â†’  /**
   198â†’   * Get the current workspace state
   199â†’   * @returns Promise resolving to workspace data
   200â†’   */
   201â†’  getWorkspace: () => Promise<Workspace>
   202â†’
   203â†’  /**
   204â†’   * Save the workspace state
   205â†’   * @param workspace Workspace data to save
   206â†’   */
   207â†’  saveWorkspace: (workspace: Workspace) => Promise<void>
   208â†’
   209â†’  // ==========================================================================
   210â†’  // Settings Management
   211â†’  // ==========================================================================
   212â†’
   213â†’  /**
   214â†’   * Get application settings
   215â†’   * @returns Promise resolving to settings
   216â†’   */
   217â†’  getSettings: () => Promise<Settings>
   218â†’
   219â†’  /**
   220â†’   * Save application settings
   221â†’   * @param settings Settings to save
   222â†’   */
   223â†’  saveSettings: (settings: Settings) => Promise<void>
   224â†’
   225â†’  // ==========================================================================
   226â†’  // Project Management
   227â†’  // ==========================================================================
   228â†’
   229â†’  /**
   230â†’   * Open a directory picker to add a project
   231â†’   * @returns Promise resolving to selected path or null
   232â†’   */
   233â†’  addProject: () => Promise<string | null>
   234â†’
   235â†’  /**
   236â†’   * Open a directory picker to select a parent folder and add all subdirectories as projects
   237â†’   * @returns Promise resolving to array of projects or null
   238â†’   */
   239â†’  addProjectsFromParent: () => Promise<Array<{ path: string; name: string }> | null>
   240â†’
   241â†’  // ==========================================================================

   244â†’
   245â†’  /**
   246â†’   * Discover recent sessions for a project
   247â†’   * @param projectPath Path to the project
   248â†’   * @param backend Optional backend type ('claude', 'gemini', 'codex', 'opencode', or 'aider')
   249â†’   */
   250â†’  discoverSessions: (projectPath: string, backend?: BackendId) => Promise<Session[]>
   251â†’
   252â†’  // ==========================================================================
   253â†’  // PTY Management
   254â†’  // ==========================================================================
   255â†’
   256â†’  /**
   257â†’   * Spawn a new PTY session
   258â†’   * @param cwd Working directory
   259â†’   * @param sessionId Optional session ID to resume
   260â†’   * @param model Optional model override
   261â†’   * @param backend Optional backend override ('claude', 'gemini', etc.)
   262â†’   */
   263â†’  spawnPty: (cwd: string, sessionId?: string, model?: string, backend?: BackendId) => Promise<string>
   264â†’
   265â†’  /**
   266â†’   * Write data to a PTY
   267â†’   */
   268â†’  writePty: (id: string, data: string) => void
   269â†’
   270â†’  /**
   271â†’   * Resize a PTY
   272â†’   */
   273â†’  resizePty: (id: string, cols: number, rows: number) => void
   274â†’
   275â†’  /**
   276â†’   * Kill a PTY
   277â†’   */
   278â†’  killPty: (id: string) => void
   279â†’
   280â†’  /**
   281â†’   * Subscribe to PTY output
   282â†’   */
   283â†’  onPtyData: (id: string, callback: (data: string) => void) => Unsubscribe
   284â†’
   285â†’  /**
   286â†’   * Subscribe to PTY exit
   287â†’   */
   288â†’  onPtyExit: (id: string, callback: (code: number) => void) => Unsubscribe
   289â†’
   290â†’  /**
   291â†’   * Switch a PTY backend (desktop only)
   292â†’   */
   293â†’  setPtyBackend?: (id: string, backend: BackendId) => Promise<void>
   294â†’
   295â†’  /**
   296â†’   * Subscribe to PTY recreated events (backend switching)
   297â†’   */
   298â†’  onPtyRecreated: (callback: (data: { oldId: string; newId: string; backend: BackendId }) => void) => Unsubscribe
   299â†’
   300â†’  // ==========================================================================
   301â†’  // TTS (Text-to-Speech)
   302â†’  // ==========================================================================
   303â†’
   304â†’  /**
   305â†’   * Install TTS instructions (CLAUDE.md markers) for a project
   306â†’   * @param projectPath Path to the project
   307â†’   * @returns Promise resolving to success status
   308â†’   */
   309â†’  ttsInstallInstructions: (projectPath: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean }>
   310â†’
   311â†’  /**
   312â†’   * Speak text using TTS
   313â†’   * @param text Text to speak
   314â†’   * @returns Promise resolving to audio data or error
   315â†’   */
   316â†’  ttsSpeak: (text: string) => Promise<{ success: boolean; audioData?: string; error?: string }>
   317â†’
   318â†’  /**
   319â†’   * Stop current TTS playback
   320â†’   * @returns Promise resolving to success status
   321â†’   */
   322â†’  ttsStop: () => Promise<{ success: boolean }>
   323â†’
   324â†’  // ==========================================================================
   325â†’  // Events
   326â†’  // ==========================================================================
   327â†’
   328â†’  /**
   329â†’   * Subscribe to API open session events (triggered by external API calls)
   330â†’   * @param callback Function called when session should be opened
   331â†’   * @returns Unsubscribe function
   332â†’   */
   333â†’  onApiOpenSession: (callback: ApiOpenSessionCallback) => Unsubscribe
   334â†’
   335â†’  /**
   336â†’   * Get connection info for external components (HTTP backend only)
   337â†’   * @returns Connection info or undefined if not applicable
   338â†’   */
   339â†’  getConnectionInfo?: () => { host: string; port: number; token: string }
   340â†’}
   341â†’
   342â†’// ============================================================================
   343â†’// Extended API Interface (Desktop-only features)
   344â†’// ============================================================================
   345â†’
   346â†’/**
   347â†’ * Extended API interface with desktop-specific features.
   348â†’ * These methods are only available in the Electron implementation
   349â†’ * and may throw or return null/undefined in the HTTP implementation.
   350â†’ */
   351â†’export interface ExtendedApi extends Api {
   352â†’  // Directory and file selection dialogs
   353â†’  selectDirectory: () => Promise<string | null>
   354â†’  selectExecutable: () => Promise<string | null>
   355â†’
   356â†’  // Window controls (Electron-only)
   357â†’  windowMinimize: () => void
   358â†’  windowMaximize: () => void
   359â†’  windowClose: () => void
   360â†’  windowIsMaximized: () => Promise<boolean>
   361â†’
   362â†’  // File utilities
   363â†’  getPathForFile: (file: File) => string
   364â†’
   365â†’  // Clipboard operations
   366â†’  readClipboardImage: () => Promise<{ success: boolean; hasImage?: boolean; path?: string; error?: string }>
   367â†’
   368â†’  // App utilities
   369â†’  getVersion: () => Promise<string>
   370â†’  isDebugMode: () => Promise<boolean>
   371â†’  refresh: () => Promise<void>
   372â†’  openExternal: (url: string) => Promise<void>
   373â†’
   374â†’  // Debug
   375â†’  debugLog: (message: string) => void
   376â†’}
   377â†’
   378â†’// ============================================================================
   379â†’// API Context Type
   380â†’// ============================================================================
   381â†’
   382â†’/**
   383â†’ * API context providing the current API instance and connection state
   384â†’ */
   385â†’export interface ApiContext {
   386â†’  /** The API implementation (Electron or HTTP) */
   387â†’  api: Api
   388â†’
   389â†’  /** The type of backend being used */
   390â†’  backendType: ApiBackendType
   391â†’
   392â†’  /** Connection state (only relevant for HTTP backend) */
   393â†’  connectionState: ConnectionState
   394â†’
   395â†’  /** Error message if connection failed */
   396â†’  connectionError?: string
   397â†’
   398â†’  /** Reconnect function for HTTP backend */
   399â†’  reconnect?: () => void
   400â†’}
   401â†’

    21â†’export function setupWebSocket(server: Server, deps: WebSocketManagerDeps): WebSocketServer {
    22â†’  const wss = new WebSocketServer({ noServer: true })
    23â†’
    24â†’  // Handle upgrade requests manually to support multiple paths
    25â†’  server.on('upgrade', (req, socket, head) => {
    26â†’    const url = new URL(req.url || '', `http://localhost:${deps.getPort()}`)
    27â†’    const pathname = url.pathname
    28â†’
    29â†’    // Get token from Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header (preferred) or query string (fallback)
    30â†’    const protocolHeader = req.headers['sec-websocket-protocol'] as string | undefined
    31â†’    const protocols = protocolHeader?.split(',').map(p => p.trim()) || []
    32â†’    const tokenFromProtocol = protocols.find(p => p.startsWith('token-'))?.slice(6)
    33â†’    const tokenFromQuery = url.searchParams.get('token')
    34â†’    const token = tokenFromProtocol || tokenFromQuery
    35â†’
    36â†’    log('WebSocket upgrade request', {
    37â†’      url: req.url,
    38â†’      pathname,
    39â†’      hasProtocolToken: !!tokenFromProtocol,
    40â†’      hasQueryToken: !!tokenFromQuery,
    41â†’      headers: {
    42â†’        host: req.headers.host,
    43â†’        origin: req.headers.origin,
    44â†’        upgrade: req.headers.upgrade
    45â†’      }
    46â†’    })
    47â†’
    48â†’    // Validate token for all WebSocket connections
    49â†’    if (token !== deps.getToken()) {
    50â†’      log('WebSocket auth failed', { providedToken: token?.slice(0, 8) })
    51â†’      socket.write('HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized\r\n\r\n')
    52â†’      socket.destroy()
    53â†’      return
    54â†’    }
    55â†’
    56â†’    // Handle PTY stream WebSocket: /api/pty/:id/stream
    57â†’    const ptyStreamMatch = pathname.match(/^\/api\/pty\/([^/]+)\/stream$/)
    58â†’    if (ptyStreamMatch) {
    59â†’      const ptyId = ptyStreamMatch[1]
    60â†’      handlePtyStreamUpgrade(req, socket, head, ptyId, deps)
    61â†’      return
    62â†’    }
    63â†’
    64â†’    // Handle main WebSocket path: /ws
    65â†’    if (pathname === '/ws') {
    66â†’      wss.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head, (ws) => {
    67â†’        wss.emit('connection', ws, req)

    69â†’      return
    70â†’    }
    71â†’
    72â†’    // Unknown WebSocket path
    73â†’    log('Unknown WebSocket path', { pathname })
    74â†’    socket.write('HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found\r\n\r\n')
    75â†’    socket.destroy()
    76â†’  })
    77â†’
    78â†’  wss.on('connection', (ws: WebSocket, _req) => {
    79â†’    log('WebSocket client connected (main)')
    80â†’
    81â†’    deps.getConnectedClients().add(ws)
    82â†’
    83â†’    ws.on('message', (message: Buffer) => {

    91â†’
    92â†’    ws.on('close', () => {

     1â†’/**
     2â†’ * WebSocket Manager - WebSocket handling
     3â†’ */
     4â†’
     5â†’import { WebSocket, WebSocketServer } from 'ws'
     6â†’import { Server } from 'http'
     7â†’import { log } from './utils'
     8â†’import { PendingFile } from './types'
     9â†’
    10â†’export interface WebSocketManagerDeps {
    11â†’  getToken: () => string
    12â†’  getPtyManager: () => any
    13â†’  getPort: () => number
    14â†’  getTerminalSubscriptions: () => Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
    15â†’  getPtyStreams: () => Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>
    16â†’  getPtyDataBuffer: () => Map<string, string[]>
    17â†’  getConnectedClients: () => Set<WebSocket>
    18â†’  getPendingFiles: () => Map<string, PendingFile>
    19â†’}
    20â†’
    21â†’export function setupWebSocket(server: Server, deps: WebSocketManagerDeps): WebSocketServer {
    22â†’  const wss = new WebSocketServer({ noServer: true })
    23â†’
    24â†’  // Handle upgrade requests manually to support multiple paths
    25â†’  server.on('upgrade', (req, socket, head) => {
    26â†’    const url = new URL(req.url || '', `http://localhost:${deps.getPort()}`)
    27â†’    const pathname = url.pathname
    28â†’
    29â†’    // Get token from Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header (preferred) or query string (fallback)
    30â†’    const protocolHeader = req.headers['sec-websocket-protocol'] as string | undefined
    31â†’    const protocols = protocolHeader?.split(',').map(p => p.trim()) || []
    32â†’    const tokenFromProtocol = protocols.find(p => p.startsWith('token-'))?.slice(6)
    33â†’    const tokenFromQuery = url.searchParams.get('token')
    34â†’    const token = tokenFromProtocol || tokenFromQuery
    35â†’
    36â†’    log('WebSocket upgrade request', {
    37â†’      url: req.url,
    38â†’      pathname,
    39â†’      hasProtocolToken: !!tokenFromProtocol,
    40â†’      hasQueryToken: !!tokenFromQuery,
    41â†’      headers: {
    42â†’        host: req.headers.host,
    43â†’        origin: req.headers.origin,
    44â†’        upgrade: req.headers.upgrade
    45â†’      }
    46â†’    })
    47â†’
    48â†’    // Validate token for all WebSocket connections
    49â†’    if (token !== deps.getToken()) {
    50â†’      log('WebSocket auth failed', { providedToken: token?.slice(0, 8) })
    51â†’      socket.write('HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized\r\n\r\n')
    52â†’      socket.destroy()
    53â†’      return
    54â†’    }
    55â†’
    56â†’    // Handle PTY stream WebSocket: /api/pty/:id/stream
    57â†’    const ptyStreamMatch = pathname.match(/^\/api\/pty\/([^/]+)\/stream$/)
    58â†’    if (ptyStreamMatch) {
    59â†’      const ptyId = ptyStreamMatch[1]
    60â†’      handlePtyStreamUpgrade(req, socket, head, ptyId, deps)
    61â†’      return
    62â†’    }
    63â†’
    64â†’    // Handle main WebSocket path: /ws
    65â†’    if (pathname === '/ws') {
    66â†’      wss.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head, (ws) => {
    67â†’        wss.emit('connection', ws, req)
    68â†’      })
    69â†’      return
    70â†’    }
    71â†’
    72â†’    // Unknown WebSocket path
    73â†’    log('Unknown WebSocket path', { pathname })
    74â†’    socket.write('HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found\r\n\r\n')
    75â†’    socket.destroy()
    76â†’  })
    77â†’
    78â†’  wss.on('connection', (ws: WebSocket, _req) => {
    79â†’    log('WebSocket client connected (main)')
    80â†’
    81â†’    deps.getConnectedClients().add(ws)
    82â†’
    83â†’    ws.on('message', (message: Buffer) => {
    84â†’      try {
    85â†’        const msg = JSON.parse(message.toString())
    86â†’        handleWebSocketMessage(ws, msg, deps)
    87â†’      } catch (e) {
    88â†’        log('Invalid WebSocket message', { error: String(e) })
    89â†’      }
    90â†’    })
    91â†’
    92â†’    ws.on('close', () => {
    93â†’      deps.getConnectedClients().delete(ws)
    94â†’
    95â†’      deps.getTerminalSubscriptions().forEach((subscribers, ptyId) => {
    96â†’        subscribers.delete(ws)
    97â†’        if (subscribers.size === 0) {
    98â†’          deps.getTerminalSubscriptions().delete(ptyId)
    99â†’        }
   100â†’      })
   101â†’      log('WebSocket client disconnected (main)')
   102â†’    })
   103â†’
   104â†’    // Send welcome message
   105â†’    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'connected', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   106â†’
   107â†’    // Send any pending files to new client
   108â†’    sendPendingFilesToClient(ws, deps.getPendingFiles())
   109â†’  })
   110â†’
   111â†’  return wss
   112â†’}
   113â†’
   114â†’function handlePtyStreamUpgrade(
   115â†’  req: any,
   116â†’  socket: any,
   117â†’  head: any,
   118â†’  ptyId: string,
   119â†’  deps: WebSocketManagerDeps
   120â†’): void {
   121â†’  const ptyManager = deps.getPtyManager()
   122â†’  if (!ptyManager || !ptyManager.getProcess(ptyId)) {
   123â†’    log('PTY stream upgrade failed - PTY not found', { ptyId })
   124â†’    socket.write('HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found\r\n\r\n')
   125â†’    socket.destroy()
   126â†’    return
   127â†’  }
   128â†’
   129â†’  const streamWss = new WebSocketServer({ noServer: true })
   130â†’
   131â†’  streamWss.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head, (ws) => {
   132â†’    log('PTY stream connected', { ptyId })
   133â†’
   134â†’    const ptyStreams = deps.getPtyStreams()
   135â†’    if (!ptyStreams.has(ptyId)) {
   136â†’      ptyStreams.set(ptyId, new Set())
   137â†’    }
   138â†’    ptyStreams.get(ptyId)!.add(ws)
   139â†’
   140â†’    ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'connected', ptyId }))
   141â†’
   142â†’    // Flush any buffered data
   143â†’    flushPtyBuffer(ptyId, ws, deps.getPtyDataBuffer())
   144â†’
   145â†’    ws.on('message', (message: Buffer) => {
   146â†’      try {
   147â†’        const msg = JSON.parse(message.toString())
   148â†’
   149â†’        switch (msg.type) {
   150â†’          case 'input':
   151â†’            if (msg.data && ptyManager) {
   152â†’              ptyManager.write(ptyId, msg.data)
   153â†’            }
   154â†’            break
   155â†’
   156â†’          case 'resize':
   157â†’            if (msg.cols && msg.rows && ptyManager) {
   158â†’              ptyManager.resize(ptyId, msg.cols, msg.rows)
   159â†’            }
   160â†’            break
   161â†’
   162â†’          case 'ping':
   163â†’            ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'pong', timestamp: Date.now() }))
   164â†’            break
   165â†’
   166â†’          default:
   167â†’            log('Unknown PTY stream message type', { type: msg.type, ptyId })
   168â†’        }
   169â†’      } catch (e) {
   170â†’        log('Invalid PTY stream message', { error: String(e), ptyId })
   171â†’      }
   172â†’    })
   173â†’
   174â†’    ws.on('close', () => {
   175â†’      log('PTY stream disconnected', { ptyId })
   176â†’      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   177â†’      if (ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.size === 0) {
   178â†’        ptyStreams.delete(ptyId)
   179â†’      }
   180â†’    })
   181â†’
   182â†’    ws.on('error', (err) => {
   183â†’      log('PTY stream error', { error: String(err), ptyId })
   184â†’      ptyStreams.get(ptyId)?.delete(ws)
   185â†’    })
   186â†’  })
   187â†’}
   188â†’
   189â†’function handleWebSocketMessage(ws: WebSocket, msg: any, deps: WebSocketManagerDeps): void {
   190â†’  const ptyManager = deps.getPtyManager()
   191â†’  const terminalSubscriptions = deps.getTerminalSubscriptions()
   192â†’
   193â†’  switch (msg.type) {
   194â†’    case 'subscribe':
   195â†’      if (msg.ptyId) {
   196â†’        if (!terminalSubscriptions.has(msg.ptyId)) {
   197â†’          terminalSubscriptions.set(msg.ptyId, new Set())
   198â†’        }
   199â†’        terminalSubscriptions.get(msg.ptyId)!.add(ws)
   200â†’        ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'subscribed', ptyId: msg.ptyId }))

    22â†’function encodeProjectPath(projectPath: string): string {
    23â†’  // Claude encodes paths by:
    24â†’  // 1. Removing trailing slashes/backslashes
    25â†’  // 2. Replacing / and \ with -
    26â†’  // 3. Replacing _ with -
    27â†’  // 4. Replacing spaces with -
    28â†’  // 5. Replacing : with - (Windows drive letters)
    29â†’  // 6. Replacing dots with dashes (e.g. .config -> -config)
    30â†’  // /home/user/my_project/ becomes -home-user-my-project
    31â†’  // /home/user/.config/app becomes -home-user--config-app
    32â†’  // C:\Users\bob\project becomes -C-Users-bob-project
    33â†’  return projectPath
    34â†’    .replace(/[/\\]+$/, '')  // Remove trailing slashes/backslashes
    35â†’    .replace(/[/\\]/g, '-')  // Replace / and \ with -
    36â†’    .replace(/:/g, '-')      // Replace : with - (Windows drive letters)
    37â†’    .replace(/_/g, '-')      // Replace _ with -
    38â†’    .replace(/ /g, '-')      // Replace spaces with -
    39â†’    .replace(/\./g, '-')     // Replace dots with dashes
    40â†’}
    41â†’
    42â†’function expandHomePath(targetPath: string): string {
    43â†’  if (targetPath === '~') {
    44â†’    return homedir()
    45â†’  }
    46â†’  if (targetPath.startsWith('~/') || targetPath.startsWith('~\\')) {
    47â†’    return join(homedir(), targetPath.slice(2))
    48â†’  }
    49â†’  return targetPath
    50â†’}
    51â†’
    52â†’function loadOpenCodeConfig(projectPath: string): { data?: { directory?: string } } | null {
    53â†’  const home = homedir()
    54â†’  const xdgConfig = process.env.XDG_CONFIG_HOME
    55â†’  const candidatePaths = [
    56â†’    join(projectPath, OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME),
    57â†’    ...(xdgConfig ? [join(xdgConfig, 'opencode', OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME)] : []),
    58â†’    join(home, '.config', 'opencode', OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME),
    59â†’    join(home, OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME)
    60â†’  ]
    61â†’
    62â†’  for (const configPath of candidatePaths) {
    63â†’    if (!existsSync(configPath)) continue
    64â†’    try {

    66â†’      return JSON.parse(content)

    69â†’    }

    21â†’
    22â†’function encodeProjectPath(projectPath: string): string {
    23â†’  // Claude encodes paths by:
    24â†’  // 1. Removing trailing slashes/backslashes
    25â†’  // 2. Replacing / and \ with -
    26â†’  // 3. Replacing _ with -
    27â†’  // 4. Replacing spaces with -
    28â†’  // 5. Replacing : with - (Windows drive letters)
    29â†’  // 6. Replacing dots with dashes (e.g. .config -> -config)
    30â†’  // /home/user/my_project/ becomes -home-user-my-project
    31â†’  // /home/user/.config/app becomes -home-user--config-app
    32â†’  // C:\Users\bob\project becomes -C-Users-bob-project
    33â†’  return projectPath
    34â†’    .replace(/[/\\]+$/, '')  // Remove trailing slashes/backslashes
    35â†’    .replace(/[/\\]/g, '-')  // Replace / and \ with -
    36â†’    .replace(/:/g, '-')      // Replace : with - (Windows drive letters)
    37â†’    .replace(/_/g, '-')      // Replace _ with -
    38â†’    .replace(/ /g, '-')      // Replace spaces with -
    39â†’    .replace(/\./g, '-')     // Replace dots with dashes
    40â†’}
    41â†’
    42â†’function expandHomePath(targetPath: string): string {
    43â†’  if (targetPath === '~') {
    44â†’    return homedir()

    47â†’    return join(homedir(), targetPath.slice(2))

    49â†’  return targetPath
    50â†’}

    59â†’    join(home, OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME)
    60â†’  ]

    62â†’  for (const configPath of candidatePaths) {

    69â†’    }
    70â†’  }
    71â†’
    72â†’  return null
    73â†’}
    74â†’

    28â†’      const modelLabel = model && model !== 'default' ? ` [${model}]` : ''
    29â†’      const title = `${projectPath.split(/[/\\]/).pop() || projectPath} - API${modelLabel}${autoClose ? ' (auto-close)' : ''}`
    30â†’
    31â†’      // Get project and determine effective backend
    32â†’      const project = projects.find((p) => p.path === projectPath)
    33â†’
    34â†’      const effectiveBackend = (project?.backend && project.backend !== 'default'
    35â†’        ? project.backend
    36â†’        : (settings?.backend && settings.backend !== 'default'
    37â†’          ? settings.backend
    38â†’          : 'claude')) as BackendId
    39â†’
    40â†’      try {
    41â†’        // Always install TTS instructions so Claude uses <tts> tags
    42â†’        await api.ttsInstallInstructions?.(projectPath)
    43â†’
    44â†’        const ptyId = await api.spawnPty(projectPath, undefined, model, effectiveBackend)
    45â†’        addTab({
    46â†’          id: ptyId,
    47â†’          projectPath,
    48â†’          title,
    49â†’          ptyId,
    50â†’          backend: effectiveBackend
    51â†’        })
    52â†’      } catch (e: any) {
    53â†’        console.error('Failed to spawn PTY for API request:', e)
    54â†’      }
    55â†’    })
    56â†’
    57â†’    return unsubscribe
    58â†’  }, [api, addTab, projects, settings?.backend])
    59â†’
    60â†’  // Listen for PTY recreation events
    61â†’  useEffect(() => {
    62â†’    const unsubscribe = api.onPtyRecreated(({ oldId, newId, backend }) => {
    63â†’      console.log(`PTY recreated: ${oldId} -> ${newId} with backend ${backend}`)
    64â†’      // Find the tab with the old ID
    65â†’      const tab = useWorkspaceStore.getState().openTabs.find((t) => t.id === oldId)
    66â†’      if (tab) {
    67â†’        // Update the tab with the new ID and backend

    69â†’        // If it was the active tab, update the active tab ID

    73â†’      }
    74â†’    })
    75â†’    return unsubscribe

    77â†’}

     4â†’import type { AppSettings } from './useSettings'
     5â†’import { useWorkspaceStore, OpenTab, Project } from '../stores/workspace'
     6â†’
     7â†’interface UseApiListenersOptions {
     8â†’  api: Api

    10â†’  settings: AppSettings | null

    24â†’  // Listen for API requests to open new sessions

    55â†’    })

    73â†’      }

## Summary: PTY Session Management, Backend Switching, and Terminal Session Lifecycle

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here's a complete inventory of all PTY-related files and potential issues:

### **1. PTY SPAWN & CREATION (node-pty)**

#### Primary File:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/pty-manager.ts`** (621 lines)
  - `class PtyManager` - Core PTY lifecycle manager
  - **Key method**: `spawn()` (lines 414-498)
    - Creates PTY using `pty.spawn()` from node-pty
    - Generates UUID for PTY ID
    - Tracks backend type and session ID
    - Registers onData and onExit listeners
    - Returns PTY ID to caller
  - **Key classes**:
    - `OutputBuffer` (lines 17-47) - Ring buffer for recent PTY output (max 200 lines)
    - `ClaudeProcess` interface (lines 49-61) - Tracks PTY metadata

#### IPC Handler:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/app/ipc-handlers/pty.ts`** (137 lines)
  - `registerPtyHandlers()` - Registers IPC endpoint `pty:spawn`
  - Resolves effective backend from global/project settings
  - Invokes `ptyManager.spawn()`
  - Sets up data/exit callbacks that send IPC events back to renderer

#### Preload Bridge:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/preload/handlers/pty.ts`** (36 lines)
  - `ptyHandlers` - Exposes: `spawnPty()`, `writePty()`, `resizePty()`, `killPty()`, `onPtyData()`, `onPtyExit()`, `onPtyRecreated()`

---

### **2. PTY RESIZE HANDLING**

#### In pty-manager.ts (lines 507-537):
- **Method**: `resize(id, cols, rows)`
- **Debouncing logic** (critical bug area):
  - Skips if dimensions unchanged
  - **Ink backend special case** (gemini): Uses 1500ms debounce for first 5s after spawn (to prevent SIGWINCH-induced re-render loops)
  - Other backends: 50ms debounce
  - Clears pending resizes before setting new timeout
- **Error handling**: Catches resize errors (PTY may have exited already)

#### Mobile/HTTP routing:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/mobile-server/routes/pty.ts`** (line 111-140)
  - POST `/api/pty/:id/resize` - Validates cols/rows (1-500 range), calls `ptyManager.resize()`

#### WebSocket streaming:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/api/http-backend/pty-websocket.ts`** (line 76)
  - Sends `{type: 'resize', cols, rows}` over WebSocket or falls back to HTTP

---

### **3. OUTPUT BUFFER / RING BUFFER IMPLEMENTATION**

#### Location: pty-manager.ts (lines 14-47)

**Class**: `OutputBuffer`
- **Data structure**: Array of strings (ANSI-stripped lines) + partial line accumulator
- **Max capacity**: 200 lines (OUTPUT_BUFFER_MAX_LINES constant)
- **Methods**:
  - `append(data)` - Splits by newlines, strips ANSI codes (2 regex patterns), maintains partial line
  - `getRecent(maxLines)` - Returns last N lines (default 50)
  - `clear()` - Resets buffer
- **Integration**: Every PTY has OutputBuffer instance; onData events feed it
- **Usage**: Orchestrator API and mobile routes use `readOutput()` to retrieve recent output

#### Potential issue:
- **Memory accumulation**: If many PTYs spawned, buffers persist in memory. Only cleaned on `cleanupProcess()` which fires on exit.

---

### **4. BACKEND SWITCHING LOGIC**

#### Main handler:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/app/ipc-handlers/pty.ts`** (lines 103-135)
  - **IPC endpoint**: `pty:set-backend` (ipcMain.handle)
  - **Flow**:
    1. Gets old PTY's process metadata (cwd, sessionId, backend)
    2. Kills old PTY
    3. Spawns new PTY with **same cwd**, **same sessionId if backend unchanged**, **new backend**
    4. Re-registers data/exit handlers
    5. Sends `pty:recreated` IPC event with oldId, newId, backend

#### Renderer handling:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/hooks/useApiListeners.ts`** (lines 61-76)
  - Listens for `api.onPtyRecreated()` event
  - Updates tab: changes `tab.id` and `tab.ptyId` to newId
  - Maintains all other tab state (projectPath, sessionId, title)
  - Sets new ID as active if it was active before

#### Terminal component:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/components/terminal/Terminal.tsx`** (lines 35-39)
  - `handleBackendChange()` callback
  - Calls `api.setPtyBackend()` or `window.electronAPI.setPtyBackend()`

#### Potential bugs:
- **Race condition**: If backend switch is called while PTY is exiting, the old exit handler may fire after new PTY is spawned, causing confusion
- **No validation**: No check that backend is actually available/installed before switching
- **Session ID loss**: When switching backends, session ID is discarded unless backend matches (line 108 in pty.ts)
- **Missing error handling**: No error callback if kill/spawn fails during backend switch

---

### **5. SESSION ID MANAGEMENT**

#### Storage:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts`** (lines 31-38)
  - `OpenTab` interface stores optional `sessionId` field
  - Persisted in workspace.json

#### Discovery:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-discovery.ts`**
  - Discovers past sessions from backend-specific storage directories
  - **Claude**: `~/.claude/projects/{encoded-path}/`
  - **Gemini**: (not shown in code, likely similar)
  - **OpenCode**: Reads `.opencode.json` config for session data dir
  - Filters sessions by conversation presence

#### Passing to PTY:
- **pty-manager.ts** (line 425):
  - `buildResumeArgs()` converts sessionId to backend-specific flags:
    - Claude: `-r {sessionId}`
    - Gemini: `--resume {sessionId}`
    - Codex: `--resume {sessionId}`
    - OpenCode: `--session {sessionId}`
    - Aider: `--restore {sessionId}`

---

### **6. PTY CLEANUP/TERMINATION**

#### Code location: pty-manager.ts (lines 539-577)

**Method**: `cleanupProcess(id)` (private, lines 539-560)
- Clears resize debounce timeout
- Disposes all event listeners
- Removes from tracking maps (processes, dataCallbacks, exitCallbacks)
- Called automatically on PTY exit

**Method**: `kill(id)` (lines 562-577)
- Sends SIGKILL on Unix, default signal on Windows
- Calls `cleanupProcess()` immediately
- Wraps in try-catch to handle already-dead processes

**Method**: `killAll()` (lines 579-585)
- Called on app shutdown and window close
- Iterates through all tracked PTYs and kills them

#### Potential issues:
- **Resource leak if cleanup fails**: try-catch in dispose() swallows errors (line 550-553), but if all disposables throw, listeners may not be fully cleaned
- **No PTY process group cleanup**: Killing parent PTY doesn't guarantee child processes are killed (handled by node-pty, but not explicitly verified)
- **WebSocket cleanup timing**: Mobile server cleanup of ptyStreams/ptyDataBuffer happens on exit event, but race condition if client disconnect happens first

---

### **7. PATH CONSTRUCTION FOR PTY PROCESSES**

#### Location: `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/pty-manager.ts`

**Function**: `getEnhancedEnv()` (lines 68-107)
- Builds enhanced PATH with portable npm/pip dirs + additional paths
- Calls `getEnhancedPathWithPortable()` from platform.ts

#### Backend-specific executable location:
- **For each backend** (claude, gemini, codex, opencode, aider):
  - Windows: Checks for .cmd/.exe in portable npm-global, then system npm paths, falls back to bare command
  - Unix: Returns bare command (relies on PATH)
  - Special case: Aider searches for aider.exe in Python Scripts directory

#### Location checks:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/platform.ts`** (lines 45-61)
  - `getAdditionalPaths()` - Returns platform-specific search paths
  - `getEnhancedPath()` - Constructs enhanced PATH
  - `getEnhancedPathWithPortable()` - Includes portable bins at front

#### Potential issues:
- **Silent fallback to bare command**: If no executable found in searched paths, just returns `'claude'` etc. and lets PATH resolve it (line 251-252). May fail cryptically if PATH doesn't have it.
- **Windows Path case-sensitivity**: Sets both `PATH` and `Path` to handle Node.js quirks (lines 76-77), but may cause issues if custom PATH vars are set
- **Git Bash assumption**: Windows assumes git-bash must be in PATH (lines 79-100), but doesn't fail gracefully if missing

---

### **8. ADDITIONAL CRITICAL FILES**

#### Mobile server PTY routing:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/mobile-server/index.ts`** (lines 66-67)
  - Tracks `ptyStreams: Map<string, Set<WebSocket>>` and `ptyDataBuffer: Map<string, string[]>`
  - PTYs spawned via HTTP route broadcast data to all connected WebSocket streams

- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/mobile-server/websocket-manager.ts`** (lines 56-187)
  - Handles `/api/pty/{id}/stream` WebSocket upgrade
  - Routes input/resize messages to ptyManager
  - Broadcasts data to all connected clients for that PTY
  - Buffering up to 1000 messages per PTY if no callbacks registered

#### HTTP API layer:
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/api/http-backend/pty-api.ts`** (lines 28-89)
  - Client-side PTY API methods
  - Spawns PTY via HTTP, connects WebSocket for data streaming
  - Write/resize operations prefer WebSocket, fall back to HTTP

- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/api/http-backend/pty-websocket.ts`** (lines 10-188)
  - Manages per-PTY WebSocket state
  - Data buffering (max 1000 messages per PTY)
  - Reconnection logic with exponential backoff (up to 5 attempts)
  - Handles 'data', 'exit', 'connected', 'pong' message types

---

### **KEY BUGS & ISSUES FOUND**

#### đź”´ **CRITICAL**

1. **Backend switch race condition** (pty.ts:103-135)
   - If PTY exit fires between kill and spawn, old PTY's data might be sent to new PTY ID
   - No transactional guarantee that oldId cleanup is complete before new handlers registered

2. **Session ID discarded on backend switch** (pty.ts:108)
   - If switching backends, session ID is set to undefined unless backend matches
   - This breaks session continuity for all backend changes
   - Should preserve sessionId for all backends

3. **No validation of backend availability before switch**
   - Code doesn't check if requested backend is installed
   - Silently falls back to bare command in PATH (findExecutable functions)
   - May fail cryptically if backend not found

#### đźźˇ **HIGH**

4. **Memory leak in OutputBuffer on app shutdown**
   - Buffers persist in memory until PTY exits
   - If app force-quit without waiting for PTY exit, OutputBuffer memory is lost (not a leak, but inefficient)
   - Mitigation: `killAll()` calls cleanupProcess immediately, so should be OK

5. **WebSocket data buffer uncapped for HTTP backend** (pty-websocket.ts:70)
   - Limits per-PTY buffer to 1000 messages
   - But if renderer doesn't register data callbacks, messages accumulate
   - Could leak memory with many PTYs and slow/disconnected clients

6. **Resize timeout not cleared on PTY death** (pty-manager.ts:543-545)
   - `cleanupProcess()` clears resize timeout
   - But if resize timeout fires after kill, it calls `proc.pty.resize()` on dead PTY
   - Protected by try-catch in resize (line 530-535), but logged as noise

#### đźź  **MEDIUM**

7. **No error propagation from backend switch**
   - `pty:set-backend` IPC handler doesn't send error back to renderer if kill/spawn fails
   - Renderer shows no error feedback to user

8. **Incomplete error handling in terminal setup** (terminalInit.ts)
   - Terminal initialization polling runs forever if container never appears
   - 50ms polling interval (line 137) never stops if container becomes null
   - Should add max retry limit or cleanup on disposal

9. **Missing PTY validation after spawn**
   - `spawn()` doesn't verify PTY is actually running before returning ID
   - If spawn fails but doesn't throw, invalid ID is returned

10. **Windows ConPTY not validated**
    - `useConpty: true` is set unconditionally on Windows (line 454)
    - No fallback if ConPTY unavailable on old Windows versions

#### đź”µ **LOW**

11. **Session discovery inefficiency** (session-discovery.ts)
    - Scans all files in session dir on every discovery call
    - No caching, could be slow for projects with many sessions
    - Parallel Promise.all() helps but still O(n)

12. **Portable deps PATH order** (platform.ts:60)
    - Portable bin dirs prepended to PATH
    - Could shadow system bins unintentionally if names collide

---

### **FILE INVENTORY (Complete List)**

| Category | File Path |
|----------|-----------|
| **PTY Core** | `/src/main/pty-manager.ts` |
| **IPC Handlers** | `/src/main/app/ipc-handlers/pty.ts` |
| **Preload Bridge** | `/src/preload/handlers/pty.ts` |
| **Session Store** | `/src/main/session-store.ts` |
| **Session Discovery** | `/src/main/session-discovery.ts` |
| **Platform Abstraction** | `/src/main/platform.ts` |
| **Portable Deps** | `/src/main/portable-deps.ts` |
| **Mobile Server Routes** | `/src/main/mobile-server/routes/pty.ts` |
| **Mobile WebSocket** | `/src/main/mobile-server/websocket-manager.ts` |
| **Mobile Server Main** | `/src/main/mobile-server/index.ts` |
| **HTTP Backend PTY API** | `/src/renderer/api/http-backend/pty-api.ts` |
| **HTTP PTY WebSocket** | `/src/renderer/api/http-backend/pty-websocket.ts` |
| **Electron Backend** | `/src/renderer/api/electron-backend.ts` |
| **API Types** | `/src/renderer/api/types.ts` |
| **Terminal Component** | `/src/renderer/components/terminal/Terminal.tsx` |
| **Terminal Setup Hook** | `/src/renderer/components/terminal/useTerminalSetup/useTerminalSetup.ts` |
| **Terminal Init** | `/src/renderer/components/terminal/useTerminalSetup/terminalInit.ts` |
| **API Listeners Hook** | `/src/renderer/hooks/useApiListeners.ts` |
| **Terminal Bar** | `/src/renderer/components/TerminalBar.tsx` |
| **Main App** | `/src/main/index.ts` |
| **App Setup** | `/src/main/app/app-setup.ts` |
